name: Cardboard Box Oven
recipeInstructions:
1. Find a corrugated cardboard box about 2 inches larger around than a cookie sheet. Make sure it has an intact bottom and top "ears".
2. Cut the bottom out all the way around and secure together with duct tape.
3. Line the inside and outside of the box with heavy-duty aluminum foil, securing seams with duct tape. Cut foil at the corners to free the "ears" for venting.
4. Line the lid with foil and secure seams with duct tape.
5. Get four wire coat hangers and snip the hook off below the twist. Straighten the wires.
6. Punch holes in the box about 6 inches from the top, then push the coat hanger wires through to create a rack.
To Use:
1. Start a charcoal fire using 6-8 briquettes in an old metal pie pan.
2. Place a piece of foil on the ground shiny side up.
3. Put the pie pan with the coals on the foil and place the oven over it.
4. Put your food to be baked on the rack and cover with the lid.
5. If you have a meat thermometer, insert it into the box just below the rack.
6. 6-8 briquettes should bring the temperature to 325-350°F. Adjust the temperature as needed before starting to bake.
